session.user:
Run the following command from the repository root /home/runner/work/erk/erk and report results:
make -n py-fast-ci 2>&1 | head -30
Also run: grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:' /home/runner/work/erk/erk/Makefile | head -40
Report what make targets are available.
session.assistant: I'll run both commands to check the make targets and see what the fast CI command would execute.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show dry-run of py-fast-ci make target

session.tool_result:
echo "=== Python Fast CI ===" && \ exit_code=0; \ echo "
--- Lint ---" && uv run ruff check || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- Format Check ---" && uv run ruff format --check || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- ty ---" && uv run ty check || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- Unit Tests (erk) ---" && uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/commands/ tests/core/ tests/real/ -n auto || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- Tests (erk-dev) ---" && uv run pytest packages/erk-dev -n auto || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- Tests (erk-statusline) ---" && uv run pytest packages/erk-statusline -n auto || exit_code=1; \ echo "
--- Tests (erk-mcp) ---" && cd packages/erk-mcp && uv run pytest tests/ -x -q && cd ../.. || exit_code=1; \ exit $exit_code

Makefile:3:prettier: Makefile:6:prettier-check: Makefile:9:format-check: Makefile:12:lint: Makefile:15:fix: Makefile:20:ty: Makefile:23:upgrade-ty: Makefile:28:test-erk-dev: Makefile:31:test-erk-mcp: Makefile:38:test-unit-erk: Makefile:43:test-integration-erk: Makefile:47:test-all-erk: test-unit-erk test-integration-erk Makefile:50:test-erk: test-unit-erk Makefile:56:test: test-unit-erk test-erk-dev Makefile:59:test-integration: test-integration-erk Makefile:62:test-all: test-all-erk test-erk-dev Makefile:64:md-check: Makefile:67:docs-check: Makefile:70:docs-validate: Makefile:73:docs-sync-check: Makefile:76:docs-fix: Makefile:79:exec-reference-check: Makefile:83:py-fast-ci: Makefile:96:fast-ci: Makefile:112:all-ci: Makefile:130:clean: Makefile:144:build: clean Makefile:148:reinstall-erk-tools: Makefile:154:publish: build Makefile:159:docs:
... [8 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! Here are the results:
## Make Targets Available
The Makefile contains 40+ targets. The main ones are:
**Code Quality:** - `prettier` - Format code with Prettier - `prettier-check` - Check Prettier formatting - `format-check` - Check all formatting - `lint` - Run linter (ruff check) - `fix` - Fix code issues - `ty` - Type checking - `upgrade-ty` - Upgrade type checker
**Testing:** - `test-erk-dev` - Test erk-dev package - `test-erk-mcp` - Test erk-mcp package - `test-unit-erk` - Unit tests for erk - `test-integration-erk` - Integration tests for erk - `test-all-erk` - All erk tests - `test-erk` - Unit tests - `test` - Unit + erk-dev tests - `test-integration` - Integration tests - `test-all` - All tests
**Documentation:** - `md-check` - Check markdown - `docs-check` - Check docs - `docs-validate` - Validate docs - `docs-sync-check` - Check docs sync - `docs-fix` - Fix docs - `docs-sync` - Sync docs - `docs` - Build docs - `docs-serve` - Serve docs - `docs-deploy` - Deploy docs
**CI:** - `py-fast-ci` - Python fast CI (linting, type checking, unit tests) - `fast-ci` - Fast CI (includes prettier check) - `all-ci` - Complete CI pipeline
**Other:** - `clean` - Clean build artifacts - `build` - Build project - `reinstall-erk-tools` - Reinstall tools - `publish` - Publish package - `mcp` - MCP support - `mcp-dev` - MCP development - `clear_impl_context` - Clear implementation context
## py-fast-ci Target Details
The `py-fast-ci` target runs a complete Python quality pipeline (dry-run output shown above):
1. **Lint** - `uv run ruff check` 2. **Format Check** - `uv run ruff format --check` 3. **Type Check** - `uv run ty check` 4. **Unit Tests** - `uv run pytest tests/unit/ tests/commands/ tests/core/ tests/real/ -n auto` 5. **erk-dev Tests** - `uv run pytest packages/erk-dev -n auto` 6. **erk-statusline Tests** - `uv run pytest packages/erk-statusline -n auto` 7. **erk-mcp Tests** - `cd packages/erk-mcp && uv run pytest tests/ -x -q`
All steps must pass (exit code 0) for the target to succeed.
